events.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - User asked whether to submit Issues or PR for Phase 1 work → decided PR - User requested creating new `feat/report-v2` branch, committing all changes, creating PR - User asked to review Phase 2 plan for bugs/issues before implementing - User accepted best-practice recommendations and said "开始" (start) to begin Phase 2 implementation - Phase 2 implementation is currently in progress 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **NodeNext ESM TypeScript** with strict mode, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` - **Git stash recovery** via dropped stash SHA (`git show ` + `git stash apply `) - **TOC generation**: h2+h3 headings, duplicate-safe slugs with `-N` suffix, `injectHeadingIds()` post-processor - **Callout syntax**: `> [!note]` → styled div with color-coded border, per-type emoji icon - **Mermaid CDN + SRI**: `hasMermaidBlocks()` scan → conditional CDN injection; `
` rendering
   - **View Transitions API**: Progressive enhancement, ~5 lines CSS + JS, cross-page fade animation
   - **Sidebar icon mode**: 768–1024px CSS only, emoji icons, `title` tooltip, `nav-label` spans hidden
   - **Sidebar collapse**: localStorage `mt-sidebar`, `body.sidebar-collapsed` CSS class
   - **Reader Mode**: `body.reader-mode` class hides sidebar/breadcrumb/meta, localStorage `mt-reader`
   - **RSS 2.0**: XML escaping with `xmlEsc()`, RFC 2822 dates, 100-item cap, graceful baseUrl omission
   - **Search filtering**: `project` field added to `SearchIndexEntry`, client + project `` dropdowns
     - Filter logic: `filterClient && e.client !== filterClient` → skip; same for project; combined AND
     ```typescript
     function lastPathSegment(p: string): string {
       return p.split(/[/\\]/).filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? ''
     }
     // renderSearchPage now has:
     var filterClient = clientSel ? clientSel.value : '';
     var filterProject = projectSel ? projectSel.value : '';
     ```

   - **Test files created:**
     - `tests/report/render/toc.test.ts` — 12 tests: extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds
     - `tests/report/render/breadcrumb.test.ts` — 7 tests
     - `tests/report/render/rss.test.ts` — 9 tests: XML validity, baseUrl behavior, escaping, 100-item limit, RFC2822

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Stash lost during branch creation**: Switched to `main` while report v1 files didn't exist there → stash pop caused `deleted by us` conflicts → did `git reset --hard HEAD` which reset the feat/report-v2 branch and `git stash drop` had already removed the stash. Fixed by discovering the stash object SHA (`93945f39764750a6f11112ebb7faa283a32d4b03`) was still in git object store → `git stash apply ` on the correct base branch recovered all changes.
   - **Branch naming conflict**: The existing branch `fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection` already had all Phase 1 report files as a committed base. Creating `feat/report-v2` from `main` (which didn't have those files) caused conflicts. Solution: create `feat/report-v2` from `fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection` instead.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Phase 2 plan had 9 issues**: P0 (Mermaid CDN decision, SPA scope, i18n conflict), P1 (RSS/OG need base URL, tag caching design, SearchIndexEntry missing project), P2 (TOC depth, breadcrumb per-page, icon source). All resolved with best-practice decisions before implementation began.
   - **`htmlShell` backward compatibility**: New `HtmlShellOptions` object needed while existing callers pass string. Solved with union type: `extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions` + `typeof` check inside function.
   - **Mermaid without CDN ban**: Resolved by conditional injection — only pages whose markdown contains ` ```mermaid ` blocks get the CDN tag, keeping all other pages CDN-free.

6. All user messages:
   - "OK，那我们是提交Issues还是提交PR呢？"
   - "新建 feat/report-v2 分支，提交所有改动，创建 PR"
   - "那现在是什么情况呢？就是我们自己的这个修改全部都提交上去了吗？没有遗漏吧？"
   - "暂时我们先还不合并，就是我们不是分了两个阶段吗？我们第二个阶段也完成了吗？"
   - "我们暂时先不合并，我们先review一下我们的这个二期的计划，看看有没有瑕疵，有没有bug。如果没有问题的话，那我们就哎做我们二期。有问题的话，我们修复我们的这个计划，然后再进行二期。"
   - "你直接给出最佳实践的方案吧，这方面我肯定不如你的优秀，你是世界上最优秀的全栈工程师。"
   - "开始"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Step 11–14 (in progress)**: Search filter (partially done), Tag system (`tags.ts` not yet created), Mermaid CDN integration into page renderers, `--locale` CLI flag in `cmd-report.ts`
   - **Step 15**: Full build + test (`npm run build && npm test`, target 620+ passing)
   - **CSS addition**: `.search-filter-select` style (referenced in search.ts HTML but CSS rule not yet added)
   - **OG meta injection**: `htmlShell` needs OG meta tags when `reportBaseUrl` is available (partially deferred)
   - **`tags.ts`**: Create tag extraction with sha256 cache + noAi fallback + AI prompt

8. Current Work:
   Implementing Phase 2, Step 11–14. The last completed action was updating `src/report/render/search.ts`:
   - Updated `buildSearchIndex()` to include `project` field using `m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd)`
   - Added `lastPathSegment()` helper function
   - Rewrote `renderSearchPage()` to include client/project `

    67→
    68→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69→// API call
    70→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    71→
    72→async function callApi(
    73→  sha256: string,
    74→  messages: SummaryMessage[],
    75→  options: SummaryOptions,
    76→): Promise<string> {
    77→  try {
    78→    // Dynamic import so the build never fails if @anthropic-ai/sdk is absent
    79→    const { default: Anthropic } = await (import('@anthropic-ai/sdk') as Promise<{ default: typeof import('@anthropic-ai/sdk').default }>)
    80→
    81→    const client = new Anthropic()
    82→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(messages)
    83→
    84→    const response = await client.messages.create({
    85→      model: options.model,
    86→      max_tokens: 200,
    87→      system:
    88→        'Summarize in 2-3 sentences. What was accomplished? What problems were solved? Be specific. Use past tense.',
    89→      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }],
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    const block = response.content[0]
    93→    const text = block && block.type === 'text' ? block.text : ''
    94→    if (text) {
    95→      writeCache(sha256, text, options.cacheDir)
    96→    }
    97→    return text
    98→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    99→    // MODULE_NOT_FOUND or any API error — never crash the build
   100→    const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code
   101→    if (code !== 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND') {
   102→      console.warn(`[summarize] API call failed: ${String(err)}`)
   103→    }
   104→    return ''
   105→  }
   106→}
   107→
   108→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→// Prompt construction
   110→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→
   112→/** First 30 messages, each truncated to 500 chars. */
   113→export function buildSummaryPrompt(messages: SummaryMessage[]): string {
   114→  const slice = messages.slice(0, 30)
   115→  const lines = slice.map(m => {
   116→    const text = m.text.slice(0, 500)
   117→    return `${m.role.toUpperCase()}: ${text}`
   118→  })
   119→  return lines.join('\n\n')
   120→}
   121→
   122→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123→// Cache read / write
   124→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   125→
   126→export function readCache(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string | null {
   127→  const path = cachePath(sha256, cacheDir)
   128→  if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   129→  try {
   130→    const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   131→    const entry = JSON.parse(raw) as SummaryCache
   132→    return entry.summary ?? null
   133→  } catch {
   134→    return null
   135→  }
   136→}
   137→
   138→export function writeCache(sha256: string, summary: string, cacheDir: string): void {
   139→  try {
   140→    mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true })
   141→    const entry: SummaryCache = {
   142→      sha256,
   143→      summary,
   144→      generated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   145→    }
   146→    writeFileSync(cachePath(sha256, cacheDir), JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   147→  } catch {
   148→    // Never crash on cache write failure
   149→  }
   150→}
   151→
   152→function cachePath(sha256: string, cacheDir: string): string {
   153→  return join(cacheDir, `${sha256}.json`)
   154→}
   155→

1→/**
     2→ * CLI adapters for `memorytree report build` and `memorytree report serve`.
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { createServer } from 'node:http'
     6→import { createReadStream, existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { extname, join, resolve, sep } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    10→// Build command
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→
    13→export interface CmdReportBuildOptions {
    14→  root: string
    15→  output: string
    16→  noAi: boolean
    17→  model: string
    18→}
    19→
    20→export async function cmdReportBuild(opts: CmdReportBuildOptions): Promise<number> {
    21→  const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
    22→  try {
    23→    await buildReport({
    24→      root: resolve(opts.root),
    25→      output: resolve(opts.output),
    26→      noAi: opts.noAi,
    27→      model: opts.model,
    28→    })
    29→    console.log(`Report generated at: ${resolve(opts.output)}`)
    30→    console.log(`Open: ${join(resolve(opts.output), 'index.html')}`)
    31→    return 0
    32→  } catch (err: unknown) {
    33→    console.error(`Report build failed: ${String(err)}`)
    34→    return 1
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→// Serve command
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→export interface CmdReportServeOptions {
    43→  dir: string
    44→  port: number
    45→}
    46→
    47→export function cmdReportServe(opts: CmdReportServeOptions): number {
    48→  const root = resolve(opts.dir)
    49→  const port = opts.port
    50→
    51→  const MIME: Record<string, string> = {
    52→    '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
    53→    '.css': 'text/css',
    54→    '.js': 'application/javascript',
    55→    '.json': 'application/json',
    56→    '.svg': 'image/svg+xml',
    57→    '.ico': 'image/x-icon',
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  const server = createServer((req, res) => {
    61→    let urlPath: string
    62→    try {
    63→      urlPath = decodeURIComponent((req.url ?? '/').split('?')[0] ?? '/')
    64→    } catch {
    65→      res.writeHead(400, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    66→      res.end('Bad request')
    67→      return
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    let filePath = join(root, urlPath)
    71→
    72→    // Containment check: reject paths that escape the serve root
    73→    // Use sep (\ on Windows, / on Unix) so the check works cross-platform.
    74→    if (!filePath.startsWith(root + sep) && filePath !== root) {
    75→      res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    76→      res.end('Forbidden')
    77→      return
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // Serve index.html for directory requests
    81→    if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) {
    82→      filePath = join(filePath, 'index.html')
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
    86→      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' })
    87→      res.end('Not found')
    88→      return
    89→    }
    90→
    91→    const ext = extname(filePath)
    92→    const contentType = MIME[ext] ?? 'application/octet-stream'
    93→    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType })
    94→    const stream = createReadStream(filePath)
    95→    stream.on('error', () => { res.destroy() })
    96→    stream.pipe(res)
    97→  })
    98→
    99→  server.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   100→    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   101→      console.error(`Error: port ${port} is already in use.`)
   102→      process.exit(1)
   103→    }
   104→    console.error(`Server error: ${String(err)}`)
   105→    process.exit(1)
   106→  })
   107→
   108→  server.listen(port, () => {
   109→    console.log(`Serving ${root}`)
   110→    console.log(`Open: http://localhost:${port}/`)
   111→    console.log('Press Ctrl+C to stop.')
   112→  })
   113→
   114→  return 0
   115→}
   116→

1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.
     3→ * Registers all subcommands via commander.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { Command } from 'commander'
     7→
     8→const program = new Command()
     9→
    10→program
    11→  .name('memorytree')
    12→  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')
    13→  .version('0.1.0')
    14→
    15→// ── init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→program
    18→  .command('init')
    19→  .description('Initialize a MemoryTree workspace in a repository')
    20→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    21→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    22→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Initial goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    23→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Template locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    24→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    25→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    26→  .option('--skip-agents', 'Deprecated — use upgrade instead')
    27→  .option('--force', 'Overwrite existing generated files')
    28→  .action(async (opts) => {
    29→    const { cmdInit } = await import('./cmd-init.js')
    30→    process.exitCode = cmdInit({
    31→      root: opts.root,

    88→      format: opts.format,
    89→    })

    91→
    92→// ── discover ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→
    94→program
    95→  .command('discover')
    96→  .description('Discover and import local AI transcripts')
    97→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    98→  .option('--client <client>', 'Client filter: all, codex, claude, gemini', 'all')
    99→  .option('--scope <scope>', 'Scope: current-project or all-projects', 'all-projects')
   100→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')

   122→  .command('locale')

   124→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')

   137→
   138→program
   139→  .command('recall')
   140→  .description('On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall')
   141→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   142→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   143→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   144→  .option('--activation-time <time>', 'ISO timestamp of session activation')
   145→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   146→  .action(async (opts) => {
   147→    const { cmdRecall } = await import('./cmd-recall.js')
   148→    process.exitCode = await cmdRecall({
   149→      root: opts.root,
   150→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   151→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   152→      activationTime: opts.activationTime ?? '',
   153→      format: opts.format,
   154→    })
   155→  })
   156→
   157→// ── report ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   158→
   159→const report = program
   160→  .command('report')
   161→  .description('Generate or serve the MemoryTree HTML report website')
   162→
   163→report
   164→  .command('build')
   165→  .description('Build a self-contained HTML report website from Memory/')
   166→  .option('--root <path>', 'Repository root (must contain Memory/)', '.')
   167→  .option(
   168→    '--output <path>',
   169→    'Output directory (default: <root>/Memory/07_reports)',
   170→    '',
   171→  )
   172→  .option('--no-ai', 'Disable AI-generated session summaries')
   173→  .option(
   174→    '--model <model>',
   175→    'Claude model for AI summaries',
   176→    'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   177→  )
   178→  .action(async (opts) => {
   179→    const { cmdReportBuild } = await import('./cmd-report.js')
   180→    const root = opts.root as string
   181→    const rawOutput = opts.output as string
   182→    const output = rawOutput || `${root}/Memory/07_reports`
   183→    // Commander's --no-ai sets opts.ai = false

   194→  .command('serve')
   195→  .description('Serve the generated report website on a local HTTP server')
   196→  .option(

   198→    'Report directory to serve (default: ./Memory/07_reports)',
   199→    './Memory/07_reports',
   200→  )
   201→  .option('--port <n>', 'Port to listen on', '4321')
   202→  .action(async (opts) => {

   204→    process.exitCode = cmdReportServe({

   215→
   216→daemon
   217→  .command('install')
   218→  .description('Register heartbeat with the OS scheduler')
   219→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override heartbeat interval (e.g., "5m")')
   220→  .option('--auto-push <bool>', 'Override auto_push setting (true/false)')
   221→  .action(async (opts) => {
   222→    const { cmdInstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   223→    process.exitCode = cmdInstall({
   224→      interval: opts.interval,
   225→      autoPush: opts.autoPush,
   226→    })
   227→  })
   228→
   229→daemon
   230→  .command('uninstall')
   231→  .description('Remove the heartbeat scheduled task')
   232→  .action(async () => {
   233→    const { cmdUninstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   234→    process.exitCode = cmdUninstall()
   235→  })
   236→
   237→daemon
   238→  .command('run-once')
   239→  .description('Execute a single heartbeat cycle now')
   240→  .action(async () => {
   241→    const { cmdRunOnce } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   242→    process.exitCode = await cmdRunOnce()
   243→  })
   244→
   245→daemon
   246→  .command('watch')
   247→  .description('Continuous heartbeat loop (development only)')
   248→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override interval')
   249→  .action(async (opts) => {
   250→    const { cmdWatch } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   251→    process.exitCode = await cmdWatch({ interval: opts.interval })
   252→  })
   253→
   254→daemon
   255→  .command('status')
   256→  .description('Show heartbeat registration and lock state')
   257→  .action(async () => {
   258→    const { cmdStatus } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   259→    process.exitCode = cmdStatus()
   260→  })
   261→
   262→program.parse()
   263→

output.text:
Now I have a thorough picture of all Phase 2 changes. Let me compile the full structured review. --- ## Phase 2 Code Review — MemoryTree Report Module Skill path: `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/` --- ### CRITICAL Issues **None found.** No raw unsanitised user-content injected into HTML, no exposed secrets, no prototype-pollution vectors, no path-traversal in the serve command. --- ### HIGH Issues #### H1 — `markdown.ts`: Inline code backticks bypass `escHtml` after bold/italic substitution File: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 211-216 The `applyInline` pipeline works like this: first `escHtml(part)` is called on the non-link text, then bold/italic/code regexes run on the already-escaped string. This means `**` and `*` and `` ` `` delimiters in user content that appear inside entity-encoded text are still substituted. The substitutions themselves are safe (``, ``, ``) but the content captured by `(.+?)` in each regex is the entity-encoded string — safe. However, there is one subtle ordering problem: the backtick replacement captures `([^`]+)` and emits `$1` where `$1` is already-escaped content. That part is fine. The real risk is that `applyInline` is called on raw heading text (line 100: `applyInline(text)` where `text` comes from `headingMatch[2]`), and `text` is _not_ pre-escaped before entering `applyInline`. Inside `applyInline`, links are extracted first (before escaping). A heading like `## [evil](javascript:alert(1))` would produce ``. The link regex (`\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)`) does not validate the href scheme — only `escHtml` is called on it, not a scheme allowlist. **Impact:** A user whose Memory files contain markdown headings (goals/knowledge/archive/todos) with `javascript:` hrefs would get an XSS vector rendered into the static site. This is a local-output-only report, so exploitability is limited, but still a correctness issue worth flagging. **Recommendation:** Add a scheme check in `applyInline` before emitting the `` tag: only allow `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, and relative URLs. --- #### H2 — `markdown.ts` / `toc.ts`: `injectHeadingIds` regex does not match headings containing inline HTML File: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, line 93 The regex `/([^<]*)<\/h[23]>/g` only matches headings whose inner content contains no `<` characters. But `markdownToHtml` (called before `injectHeadingIds`) may output headings like `

Bold Title

` if bold markup is in the heading. The `[^<]*` pattern fails to match, so no `id` attribute is injected and the TOC anchor links produce dead `#section-one` hrefs. **Impact:** TOC links silently fail to scroll for any heading that contains bold, italic, code, or link inline formatting. **Recommendation:** Change the inner capture group to `([\s\S]*?)` (non-greedy) or `([^]*?)`, and update the matching logic to strip inline tags from the inner content before comparing with `escHtml(e.text)`. --- #### H3 — `tags.ts`: No validation of individual tag string length or content File: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 83-87 Tags are filtered `typeof t === 'string' && t.length > 0` but there is no upper bound on individual tag length. A model could return a 500-character "tag" string. After `toLowerCase()` and `trim()`, this string lands in `data-tag="${escTag(t)}"` and as visible text in the DOM. HTML-escaping is present (`escTag`), so no XSS. But a very long tag (e.g. 500 chars) would break the layout and inflate the search index unnecessarily. **Recommendation:** Add `.filter(t => t.length <= 50)` or similar after the trim step. --- ### MEDIUM Issues #### M1 — `toc.ts`: Duplicate ID algorithm is off-by-one for three or more identical headings File: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, lines 41-43 ``` const count = seen.get(base) ?? 0 const id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}` seen.set(base, count + 1) ``` For three identical headings `## Intro`, the IDs generated are: - 1st: count=0 → id=`intro`, stored count=1 - 2nd: count=1 → id=`intro-1`, stored count=2 - 3rd: count=2 → id=`intro-2`, stored count=3 This is correct for uniqueness. The tests cover this case and pass (lines 24-26 of `toc.test.ts`). However, the suffix counter diverges from GitHub-Flavored Markdown's convention which starts at `-1` for the first duplicate. The docs comment says `-1, -2, …` so the intent is intentional, but there is a discrepancy: the first _original_ heading gets no suffix, the first _duplicate_ gets `-1`, the second duplicate gets `-2`. This is consistent and correct per the documented intent. No bug, but worth noting the divergence from GFM (which would put `-1` on the second occurrence, `-2` on the third) — that's exactly what this code does. Actually the code is correct; this is a non-issue on closer inspection. #### M2 — `rss.ts`: `toRfc2822` silently falls back to `new Date()` on invalid ISO File: `src/report/render/rss.ts`, lines 83-88 ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { try { return new Date(iso).toUTCString() } catch { return new Date().toUTCString() } } ``` `new Date('invalid')` does not throw — it returns an Invalid Date object. `new Date('invalid').toUTCString()` returns the string `"Invalid Date"` in V8, not a valid RFC 2822 date string. This will produce a malformed `` element that RSS readers may reject for the entire feed if they are strict. **Recommendation:** Check `isNaN(d.getTime())` after construction and fall back to `new Date().toUTCString()` in that branch. --- #### M3 — `search.ts`: `project` field in `buildSearchIndex` uses `lastPathSegment(m.cwd)` when `m.project` is empty, but `m.cwd` can be empty string File: `src/report/render/search.ts`, lines 27-28 ```ts const project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown' ``` `lastPathSegment('')` returns `''` (the `filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? ''` chain), so this correctly falls back to `'unknown'`. No crash. However, if `m.cwd` is `'.'` or `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `'.'` or `''` respectively. The string `'.'` then appears as a project filter dropdown option, which is confusing UX. --- #### M4 — `layout.ts`: `htmlShell` dual-signature design creates `lang` parameter precedence ambiguity File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 289-300 ```ts export function htmlShell( title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '', lang = 'en', ): string { const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string' ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang } // ← uses the positional `lang` param : extraHeadOrOpts // ← uses opts.lang if present const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang ``` When called as `htmlShell(title, content, nav, { extraHead: '...' })` (without `lang`), `opts.lang` is `undefined`, and `resolvedLang` falls back to the positional `lang = 'en'` default. That is correct. But when called with the legacy string form `htmlShell(title, content, nav, '', 'zh-CN')`, the object created is `{ extraHead: '...', lang: 'zh-CN' }`, so `resolvedLang = 'zh-CN'`. Also correct. The design works, but it is fragile: a call site could accidentally pass both a `HtmlShellOptions.lang` and a fifth positional argument and the positional one would be ignored. This is a maintainability concern. --- #### M5 — `tags.ts`: Concurrent writes to the same cache file are not atomic File: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 136-145 `writeFileSync` is called from multiple concurrent `Promise.all` invocations in `build.ts` (line 144-149). Two concurrent sessions with the same `sha256` (which would be unusual but possible if manifests share a raw file) could race on `writeTagCache`. On most OS/Node.js combinations `writeFileSync` is not atomic — a partial write would leave a truncated JSON file. On the next read, the corrupt file would be caught by the `try/catch` in `readTagCache` and return `null`, causing a redundant API call, which is acceptable. But the corrupt file remains on disk. **Impact:** Low in practice (sha256 collisions between manifest entries are uncommon), but the pattern is fragile. This is the same pattern used by `summarize.ts`'s cache, which was accepted in Phase 1. --- #### M6 — `build.test.ts`: Integration test passes `pdf: false` which is not in `BuildReportOptions` type File: `tests/report/build.test.ts`, lines 175-180 ```ts await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false, // ← not a field in BuildReportOptions }) ``` `BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript strict mode should flag this as an error unless the type is cast or `pdf` is a known extra key. This indicates either the test predates a type change, or `tsconfig` allows excess properties in some build step. This should cause a type-check failure under `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`. **Recommendation:** Remove `pdf: false` from the test call. --- ### LOW Issues #### L1 — `markdown.ts`: YAML front matter detection only fires on the very first line (`i === 0`) File: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 87-92 ```ts if (i === 0 && line === '---') { ``` This skips front matter correctly only if `---` is the literal first line. If the markdown file has a BOM or any leading whitespace, front matter is not stripped and `---` appears as a horizontal rule. The `parseCleanMarkdownMessages` in `build.ts` processes clean markdown, which should be normalised. Low risk in practice. --- #### L2 — `layout.ts` inline JS: `document.startViewTransition` intercepts all relative `` clicks, including anchor-only links File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 100-109 ```js if (!href || href.startsWith('#') || href.startsWith('http')) return; ``` The guard correctly exempts `#` fragments and `http` URLs. However it does not exempt `https:` URLs (only `http:`). An absolute `https://` link in rendered content would be intercepted and navigated via `window.location.href = href`. The View Transitions API then fires a cross-origin navigation, which is a no-op for the transition but still sets `window.location`. This is harmless but unexpected. **Recommendation:** Change the check to `href.startsWith('http')` already covers both schemes (`http://` and `https://` both start with `http`). On re-reading, `'https://'.startsWith('http')` is `true`. So this is actually fine — no issue. --- #### L3 — `cmd-report.ts` serve command: `statSync` called after `existsSync`, TOCTOU race File: `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, lines 85-87 ```ts if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) { ``` Classic TOCTOU: the file could be deleted between `existsSync` and `statSync`. The `statSync` would throw. The `stream.on('error', ...)` handler below would not catch a sync `statSync` throw. However the server is a local dev tool serving a static generated directory, so exploitability is nil. Nonetheless it would cause an unhandled exception and crash the server process. **Recommendation:** Wrap in a try/catch or combine into a single `statSync` with try/catch. --- #### L4 — `layout.ts`: Reader Mode toggle always renders (even on pages where it adds no value) File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, line 303 ```ts const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false ? `` : '' ``` The default is to render the button unless `readerMode` is explicitly set to `false`. The dashboard and search pages emit the button even though they have no prose content to read. This is cosmetic but slightly misleading UX. --- #### L5 — `rss.ts`: Missing `` link — RSS best practice violation File: `src/report/render/rss.ts` The feed declares `xmlns:atom` namespace but never emits an `` element, which is required by the Atom extension for feed validators and feed autodiscovery. This will cause Atom-aware validators to warn. Not a functional bug. --- #### L6 — `tags.ts`: `renderTagBadges` is exported from `tags.ts` but also re-exported via `build.ts` import File: `src/report/build.ts`, line 36 ```ts import { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js' ``` `renderTagBadges` is imported in `build.ts` but never used there — `transcript-list.ts` imports it directly from `tags.ts`. This is a dead import in `build.ts`. --- #### L7 — `search.ts`: `project` field is always set in `SearchIndexEntry` but can be empty string `''` after `lastPathSegment` As discussed in M3, when `m.project` is empty and `m.cwd` is `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `''`. Then the search index entry has `project: ''` (not `'unknown'` because `'' || 'unknown'` short-circuits to `'unknown'` — actually this does work correctly). Rechecked: `'' || 'unknown'` = `'unknown'`, so this path is safe. Withdraw — not actually an issue. --- ### Test Coverage Gaps | Area | Coverage Status | |---|---| | `markdown.ts` callout rendering | **Missing** — no test for `> [!note]`, `> [!warning]`, etc. | | `markdown.ts` mermaid block detection (`hasMermaidBlocks`) | **Missing** — not tested at all | | `markdown.ts` `applyInline` link with `javascript:` href | **Missing** — no XSS regression test | | `search.ts` filter dropdowns (client + project) | **Missing** — `renderSearchPage` tests do not assert dropdown HTML | | `build.ts` `feed.xml` is created | **Missing** — integration test checks HTML files but not `feed.xml` | | `build.ts` `reportBaseUrl` passed through to RSS | **Missing** | | `config.ts` `report_base_url` field | **Missing** — not tested in `config.test.ts` | | `tags.ts` malformed cache JSON (corrupted file) | Partially covered — `readTagCache` has try/catch but no explicit test | | `toc.ts` heading with inline HTML (`**bold**` in heading text) | **Missing** — content.text:
Now I have a thorough picture of all Phase 2 changes. Let me compile the full structured review. --- ## Phase 2 Code Review — MemoryTree Report Module Skill path: `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/` --- ### CRITICAL Issues **None found.** No raw unsanitised user-content injected into HTML, no exposed secrets, no prototype-pollution vectors, no path-traversal in the serve command. --- ### HIGH Issues #### H1 — `markdown.ts`: Inline code backticks bypass `escHtml` after bold/italic substitution File: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 211-216 The `applyInline` pipeline works like this: first `escHtml(part)` is called on the non-link text, then bold/italic/code regexes run on the already-escaped string. This means `**` and `*` and `` ` `` delimiters in user content that appear inside entity-encoded text are still substituted. The substitutions themselves are safe (``, ``, ``) but the content captured by `(.+?)` in each regex is the entity-encoded string — safe. However, there is one subtle ordering problem: the backtick replacement captures `([^`]+)` and emits `$1` where `$1` is already-escaped content. That part is fine. The real risk is that `applyInline` is called on raw heading text (line 100: `applyInline(text)` where `text` comes from `headingMatch[2]`), and `text` is _not_ pre-escaped before entering `applyInline`. Inside `applyInline`, links are extracted first (before escaping). A heading like `## [evil](javascript:alert(1))` would produce ``. The link regex (`\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)`) does not validate the href scheme — only `escHtml` is called on it, not a scheme allowlist. **Impact:** A user whose Memory files contain markdown headings (goals/knowledge/archive/todos) with `javascript:` hrefs would get an XSS vector rendered into the static site. This is a local-output-only report, so exploitability is limited, but still a correctness issue worth flagging. **Recommendation:** Add a scheme check in `applyInline` before emitting the `` tag: only allow `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, and relative URLs. --- #### H2 — `markdown.ts` / `toc.ts`: `injectHeadingIds` regex does not match headings containing inline HTML File: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, line 93 The regex `/([^<]*)<\/h[23]>/g` only matches headings whose inner content contains no `<` characters. But `markdownToHtml` (called before `injectHeadingIds`) may output headings like `

Bold Title

` if bold markup is in the heading. The `[^<]*` pattern fails to match, so no `id` attribute is injected and the TOC anchor links produce dead `#section-one` hrefs. **Impact:** TOC links silently fail to scroll for any heading that contains bold, italic, code, or link inline formatting. **Recommendation:** Change the inner capture group to `([\s\S]*?)` (non-greedy) or `([^]*?)`, and update the matching logic to strip inline tags from the inner content before comparing with `escHtml(e.text)`. --- #### H3 — `tags.ts`: No validation of individual tag string length or content File: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 83-87 Tags are filtered `typeof t === 'string' && t.length > 0` but there is no upper bound on individual tag length. A model could return a 500-character "tag" string. After `toLowerCase()` and `trim()`, this string lands in `data-tag="${escTag(t)}"` and as visible text in the DOM. HTML-escaping is present (`escTag`), so no XSS. But a very long tag (e.g. 500 chars) would break the layout and inflate the search index unnecessarily. **Recommendation:** Add `.filter(t => t.length <= 50)` or similar after the trim step. --- ### MEDIUM Issues #### M1 — `toc.ts`: Duplicate ID algorithm is off-by-one for three or more identical headings File: `src/report/render/toc.ts`, lines 41-43 ``` const count = seen.get(base) ?? 0 const id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}` seen.set(base, count + 1) ``` For three identical headings `## Intro`, the IDs generated are: - 1st: count=0 → id=`intro`, stored count=1 - 2nd: count=1 → id=`intro-1`, stored count=2 - 3rd: count=2 → id=`intro-2`, stored count=3 This is correct for uniqueness. The tests cover this case and pass (lines 24-26 of `toc.test.ts`). However, the suffix counter diverges from GitHub-Flavored Markdown's convention which starts at `-1` for the first duplicate. The docs comment says `-1, -2, …` so the intent is intentional, but there is a discrepancy: the first _original_ heading gets no suffix, the first _duplicate_ gets `-1`, the second duplicate gets `-2`. This is consistent and correct per the documented intent. No bug, but worth noting the divergence from GFM (which would put `-1` on the second occurrence, `-2` on the third) — that's exactly what this code does. Actually the code is correct; this is a non-issue on closer inspection. #### M2 — `rss.ts`: `toRfc2822` silently falls back to `new Date()` on invalid ISO File: `src/report/render/rss.ts`, lines 83-88 ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { try { return new Date(iso).toUTCString() } catch { return new Date().toUTCString() } } ``` `new Date('invalid')` does not throw — it returns an Invalid Date object. `new Date('invalid').toUTCString()` returns the string `"Invalid Date"` in V8, not a valid RFC 2822 date string. This will produce a malformed `` element that RSS readers may reject for the entire feed if they are strict. **Recommendation:** Check `isNaN(d.getTime())` after construction and fall back to `new Date().toUTCString()` in that branch. --- #### M3 — `search.ts`: `project` field in `buildSearchIndex` uses `lastPathSegment(m.cwd)` when `m.project` is empty, but `m.cwd` can be empty string File: `src/report/render/search.ts`, lines 27-28 ```ts const project = m.project || lastPathSegment(m.cwd) || 'unknown' ``` `lastPathSegment('')` returns `''` (the `filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? ''` chain), so this correctly falls back to `'unknown'`. No crash. However, if `m.cwd` is `'.'` or `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `'.'` or `''` respectively. The string `'.'` then appears as a project filter dropdown option, which is confusing UX. --- #### M4 — `layout.ts`: `htmlShell` dual-signature design creates `lang` parameter precedence ambiguity File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 289-300 ```ts export function htmlShell( title: string, content: string, nav: string, extraHeadOrOpts: string | HtmlShellOptions = '', lang = 'en', ): string { const opts: HtmlShellOptions = typeof extraHeadOrOpts === 'string' ? { extraHead: extraHeadOrOpts, lang } // ← uses the positional `lang` param : extraHeadOrOpts // ← uses opts.lang if present const resolvedLang = opts.lang ?? lang ``` When called as `htmlShell(title, content, nav, { extraHead: '...' })` (without `lang`), `opts.lang` is `undefined`, and `resolvedLang` falls back to the positional `lang = 'en'` default. That is correct. But when called with the legacy string form `htmlShell(title, content, nav, '', 'zh-CN')`, the object created is `{ extraHead: '...', lang: 'zh-CN' }`, so `resolvedLang = 'zh-CN'`. Also correct. The design works, but it is fragile: a call site could accidentally pass both a `HtmlShellOptions.lang` and a fifth positional argument and the positional one would be ignored. This is a maintainability concern. --- #### M5 — `tags.ts`: Concurrent writes to the same cache file are not atomic File: `src/report/tags.ts`, lines 136-145 `writeFileSync` is called from multiple concurrent `Promise.all` invocations in `build.ts` (line 144-149). Two concurrent sessions with the same `sha256` (which would be unusual but possible if manifests share a raw file) could race on `writeTagCache`. On most OS/Node.js combinations `writeFileSync` is not atomic — a partial write would leave a truncated JSON file. On the next read, the corrupt file would be caught by the `try/catch` in `readTagCache` and return `null`, causing a redundant API call, which is acceptable. But the corrupt file remains on disk. **Impact:** Low in practice (sha256 collisions between manifest entries are uncommon), but the pattern is fragile. This is the same pattern used by `summarize.ts`'s cache, which was accepted in Phase 1. --- #### M6 — `build.test.ts`: Integration test passes `pdf: false` which is not in `BuildReportOptions` type File: `tests/report/build.test.ts`, lines 175-180 ```ts await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false, // ← not a field in BuildReportOptions }) ``` `BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript strict mode should flag this as an error unless the type is cast or `pdf` is a known extra key. This indicates either the test predates a type change, or `tsconfig` allows excess properties in some build step. This should cause a type-check failure under `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`. **Recommendation:** Remove `pdf: false` from the test call. --- ### LOW Issues #### L1 — `markdown.ts`: YAML front matter detection only fires on the very first line (`i === 0`) File: `src/report/render/markdown.ts`, lines 87-92 ```ts if (i === 0 && line === '---') { ``` This skips front matter correctly only if `---` is the literal first line. If the markdown file has a BOM or any leading whitespace, front matter is not stripped and `---` appears as a horizontal rule. The `parseCleanMarkdownMessages` in `build.ts` processes clean markdown, which should be normalised. Low risk in practice. --- #### L2 — `layout.ts` inline JS: `document.startViewTransition` intercepts all relative `` clicks, including anchor-only links File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, lines 100-109 ```js if (!href || href.startsWith('#') || href.startsWith('http')) return; ``` The guard correctly exempts `#` fragments and `http` URLs. However it does not exempt `https:` URLs (only `http:`). An absolute `https://` link in rendered content would be intercepted and navigated via `window.location.href = href`. The View Transitions API then fires a cross-origin navigation, which is a no-op for the transition but still sets `window.location`. This is harmless but unexpected. **Recommendation:** Change the check to `href.startsWith('http')` already covers both schemes (`http://` and `https://` both start with `http`). On re-reading, `'https://'.startsWith('http')` is `true`. So this is actually fine — no issue. --- #### L3 — `cmd-report.ts` serve command: `statSync` called after `existsSync`, TOCTOU race File: `src/cli/cmd-report.ts`, lines 85-87 ```ts if (existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isDirectory()) { ``` Classic TOCTOU: the file could be deleted between `existsSync` and `statSync`. The `statSync` would throw. The `stream.on('error', ...)` handler below would not catch a sync `statSync` throw. However the server is a local dev tool serving a static generated directory, so exploitability is nil. Nonetheless it would cause an unhandled exception and crash the server process. **Recommendation:** Wrap in a try/catch or combine into a single `statSync` with try/catch. --- #### L4 — `layout.ts`: Reader Mode toggle always renders (even on pages where it adds no value) File: `src/report/render/layout.ts`, line 303 ```ts const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false ? `` : '' ``` The default is to render the button unless `readerMode` is explicitly set to `false`. The dashboard and search pages emit the button even though they have no prose content to read. This is cosmetic but slightly misleading UX. --- #### L5 — `rss.ts`: Missing `` link — RSS best practice violation File: `src/report/render/rss.ts` The feed declares `xmlns:atom` namespace but never emits an `` element, which is required by the Atom extension for feed validators and feed autodiscovery. This will cause Atom-aware validators to warn. Not a functional bug. --- #### L6 — `tags.ts`: `renderTagBadges` is exported from `tags.ts` but also re-exported via `build.ts` import File: `src/report/build.ts`, line 36 ```ts import { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js' ``` `renderTagBadges` is imported in `build.ts` but never used there — `transcript-list.ts` imports it directly from `tags.ts`. This is a dead import in `build.ts`. --- #### L7 — `search.ts`: `project` field is always set in `SearchIndexEntry` but can be empty string `''` after `lastPathSegment` As discussed in M3, when `m.project` is empty and `m.cwd` is `'/'`, `lastPathSegment` returns `''`. Then the search index entry has `project: ''` (not `'unknown'` because `'' || 'unknown'` short-circuits to `'unknown'` — actually this does work correctly). Rechecked: `'' || 'unknown'` = `'unknown'`, so this path is safe. Withdraw — not actually an issue. --- ### Test Coverage Gaps | Area | Coverage Status | |---|---| | `markdown.ts` callout rendering | **Missing** — no test for `> [!note]`, `> [!warning]`, etc. | | `markdown.ts` mermaid block detection (`hasMermaidBlocks`) | **Missing** — not tested at all | | `markdown.ts` `applyInline` link with `javascript:` href | **Missing** — no XSS regression test | | `search.ts` filter dropdowns (client + project) | **Missing** — `renderSearchPage` tests do not assert dropdown HTML | | `build.ts` `feed.xml` is created | **Missing** — integration test checks HTML files but not `feed.xml` | | `build.ts` `reportBaseUrl` passed through to RSS | **Missing** | | `config.ts` `report_base_url` field | **Missing** — not tested in `config.test.ts` | | `tags.ts` output.text:
Now I have a complete picture of all the files. Let me compile the full second-pass review. --- ## Second-Pass Code Review — Phase 2 Changes (MemoryTree Report Module) ### Issue 1 — CRITICAL: `build.ts` imports `renderTagBadges` but never uses it **File:** `src/report/build.ts` line 36 ```ts import { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js' ``` `renderTagBadges` is never called anywhere in `build.ts`. The actual rendering is done inside `src/report/render/transcript-list.ts` (which has its own import of `renderTagBadges` from `../tags.js`). Under `tsup` / `noUnusedLocals` the TypeScript compiler may not catch this because tsup bundles without strict checking by default. **Impact:** Dead import; also suggests a design concern — `build.ts` imported `renderTagBadges` thinking it would orchestrate tag-badge rendering, but the renderer is handling it directly. If `renderTagBadges` is later removed from `tags.ts`, `build.ts` will fail to compile. **Fix:** Remove `renderTagBadges` from the import on line 36: `import { getTags } from './tags.js'` --- ### Issue 2 — CRITICAL: `build.test.ts` passes `pdf: false` which does not exist on `BuildReportOptions` **File:** `tests/report/build.test.ts` lines 179, 193, 210, 223 ```ts await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false, // ← not in BuildReportOptions }) ``` `BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript's `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` doesn't cause this to fail, but under strict mode with `noExcessPropertyChecks` active (which TypeScript always applies for object literals) this **should** be a compile-time error. If the test currently passes, it's because tsup or vitest is running without strict type-checking, silently ignoring the extra key. This is a latent type error that will surface if stricter compilation is introduced. **Fix:** Remove `pdf: false` from all four `buildReport()` calls in `build.test.ts`. --- ### Issue 3 — HIGH: `rss.ts` — `new Date('invalid')` does not throw; the `try/catch` in `toRfc2822` is ineffective **File:** `src/report/render/rss.ts` lines 82–88 ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { try { return new Date(iso).toUTCString() } catch { return new Date().toUTCString() } } ``` In Node.js, `new Date('invalid-string')` does **not** throw an exception — it returns an `Invalid Date` object. Calling `.toUTCString()` on an `Invalid Date` returns the string `"Invalid Date"`, which is not valid RFC 2822. The `catch` block is therefore unreachable dead code and provides no protection. This means any manifest with a malformed `started_at` will silently produce `Invalid Date` in the RSS feed, which RSS validators and feed readers will reject at the item level. **Fix:** Check `isNaN(date.getTime())` before calling `.toUTCString()`: ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { const d = new Date(iso) return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? new Date().toUTCString() : d.toUTCString() } ``` The `rss.test.ts` suite has no test for an invalid/malformed date string, leaving this path completely untested. --- ### Issue 4 — HIGH: `toc.ts` — `injectHeadingIds` regex `[^<]*` fails to match headings with inline HTML (bold/italic) **File:** `src/report/render/toc.ts` line 93 ```ts return html.replace(/([^<]*)<\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => { ``` The regex `([^<]*)` captures only text content with no `<` characters. However, `markdownToHtml` applies inline formatting to heading text via `applyInline()`, which turns `**bold**` into `bold` and `` `code` `` into `code`. Any heading containing bold, italic, or inline code will produce HTML like: ```html

Overview

``` This heading will **not** be matched by the regex (the `<` in `` breaks `[^<]*`), so no `id` attribute gets injected, and the TOC anchor link becomes a dead link. The `toc.test.ts` tests only use plain-text headings (`'Section One'`, `'Sub Section'`), so this path is not tested. The `injectHeadingIds` test case "leaves unmatched headings unchanged" (line 95–100) does not test the inline-HTML case. **Fix:** Widen the capture group from `[^<]*` to `[^]*?` (or `[\s\S]*?`) to allow any inner HTML: ```ts return html.replace(/([\s\S]*?)<\/h[23]>/g, ...) ``` The matching logic comparing `escHtml(e.text)` to `innerHtml.trim()` also needs to account for stripped HTML tags when comparing. This is a deeper design issue: the plain-text extracted by `extractToc` (which reads raw markdown) will not match the HTML-rendered text. A robust fix would be to strip HTML tags from `innerHtml` before comparison. --- ### Issue 5 — HIGH: Inter-module inconsistency — `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` skip TOC injection that `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` include **Files:** `src/report/render/todos.ts`, `src/report/render/archive.ts` vs `src/report/render/knowledge.ts`, `src/report/render/goals.ts` `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` both: 1. Import `extractToc`, `renderToc`, `injectHeadingIds` from `./toc.js` 2. Generate per-file TOC navigation 3. Inject heading IDs into the rendered HTML `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` do **neither**: - They do not import from `./toc.js` at all - They call `markdownToHtml(f.content)` directly with no `injectHeadingIds` - No sticky TOC is rendered This is architecturally inconsistent — users can navigate headings in Knowledge and Goals files but not in Archive and Todos files, despite the pages having identical card-based layouts. If Archive or Todo files contain long markdown with many headings, the missing TOC makes them harder to navigate. **Fix:** Either add TOC injection to `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` (matching the `knowledge.ts` pattern), or explicitly document the decision to omit TOC from those pages. --- ### Issue 6 — MEDIUM: `config.test.ts` has no test for `report_base_url` field **File:** `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` The `Config` interface in `src/heartbeat/config.ts` was extended with `report_base_url: string` (line 52), a default constant `DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''` (line 27), and parsing logic (lines 251–254). However, `config.test.ts` has comprehensive test sections for `generate_report`/`ai_summary_model` (lines 274–325) and for `locale`/`gh_pages_branch`/`cname`/`webhook_url` (lines 331–418), but **no corresponding test section for `report_base_url`**. Now I have a complete picture of all the files. Let me compile the full second-pass review. --- ## Second-Pass Code Review — Phase 2 Changes (MemoryTree Report Module) ### Issue 1 — CRITICAL: `build.ts` imports `renderTagBadges` but never uses it **File:** `src/report/build.ts` line 36 ```ts import { getTags, renderTagBadges } from './tags.js' ``` `renderTagBadges` is never called anywhere in `build.ts`. The actual rendering is done inside `src/report/render/transcript-list.ts` (which has its own import of `renderTagBadges` from `../tags.js`). Under `tsup` / `noUnusedLocals` the TypeScript compiler may not catch this because tsup bundles without strict checking by default. **Impact:** Dead import; also suggests a design concern — `build.ts` imported `renderTagBadges` thinking it would orchestrate tag-badge rendering, but the renderer is handling it directly. If `renderTagBadges` is later removed from `tags.ts`, `build.ts` will fail to compile. **Fix:** Remove `renderTagBadges` from the import on line 36: `import { getTags } from './tags.js'` --- ### Issue 2 — CRITICAL: `build.test.ts` passes `pdf: false` which does not exist on `BuildReportOptions` **File:** `tests/report/build.test.ts` lines 179, 193, 210, 223 ```ts await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false, // ← not in BuildReportOptions }) ``` `BuildReportOptions` (in `src/types/report.ts`) has no `pdf` field. TypeScript's `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` doesn't cause this to fail, but under strict mode with `noExcessPropertyChecks` active (which TypeScript always applies for object literals) this **should** be a compile-time error. If the test currently passes, it's because tsup or vitest is running without strict type-checking, silently ignoring the extra key. This is a latent type error that will surface if stricter compilation is introduced. **Fix:** Remove `pdf: false` from all four `buildReport()` calls in `build.test.ts`. --- ### Issue 3 — HIGH: `rss.ts` — `new Date('invalid')` does not throw; the `try/catch` in `toRfc2822` is ineffective **File:** `src/report/render/rss.ts` lines 82–88 ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { try { return new Date(iso).toUTCString() } catch { return new Date().toUTCString() } } ``` In Node.js, `new Date('invalid-string')` does **not** throw an exception — it returns an `Invalid Date` object. Calling `.toUTCString()` on an `Invalid Date` returns the string `"Invalid Date"`, which is not valid RFC 2822. The `catch` block is therefore unreachable dead code and provides no protection. This means any manifest with a malformed `started_at` will silently produce `Invalid Date` in the RSS feed, which RSS validators and feed readers will reject at the item level. **Fix:** Check `isNaN(date.getTime())` before calling `.toUTCString()`: ```ts function toRfc2822(iso: string): string { const d = new Date(iso) return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? new Date().toUTCString() : d.toUTCString() } ``` The `rss.test.ts` suite has no test for an invalid/malformed date string, leaving this path completely untested. --- ### Issue 4 — HIGH: `toc.ts` — `injectHeadingIds` regex `[^<]*` fails to match headings with inline HTML (bold/italic) **File:** `src/report/render/toc.ts` line 93 ```ts return html.replace(/([^<]*)<\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => { ``` The regex `([^<]*)` captures only text content with no `<` characters. However, `markdownToHtml` applies inline formatting to heading text via `applyInline()`, which turns `**bold**` into `bold` and `` `code` `` into `code`. Any heading containing bold, italic, or inline code will produce HTML like: ```html

Overview

``` This heading will **not** be matched by the regex (the `<` in `` breaks `[^<]*`), so no `id` attribute gets injected, and the TOC anchor link becomes a dead link. The `toc.test.ts` tests only use plain-text headings (`'Section One'`, `'Sub Section'`), so this path is not tested. The `injectHeadingIds` test case "leaves unmatched headings unchanged" (line 95–100) does not test the inline-HTML case. **Fix:** Widen the capture group from `[^<]*` to `[^]*?` (or `[\s\S]*?`) to allow any inner HTML: ```ts return html.replace(/([\s\S]*?)<\/h[23]>/g, ...) ``` The matching logic comparing `escHtml(e.text)` to `innerHtml.trim()` also needs to account for stripped HTML tags when comparing. This is a deeper design issue: the plain-text extracted by `extractToc` (which reads raw markdown) will not match the HTML-rendered text. A robust fix would be to strip HTML tags from `innerHtml` before comparison. --- ### Issue 5 — HIGH: Inter-module inconsistency — `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` skip TOC injection that `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` include **Files:** `src/report/render/todos.ts`, `src/report/render/archive.ts` vs `src/report/render/knowledge.ts`, `src/report/render/goals.ts` `knowledge.ts` and `goals.ts` both: 1. Import `extractToc`, `renderToc`, `injectHeadingIds` from `./toc.js` 2. Generate per-file TOC navigation 3. Inject heading IDs into the rendered HTML `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` do **neither**: - They do not import from `./toc.js` at all - They call `markdownToHtml(f.content)` directly with no `injectHeadingIds` - No sticky TOC is rendered This is architecturally inconsistent — users can navigate headings in Knowledge and Goals files but not in Archive and Todos files, despite the pages having identical card-based layouts. If Archive or Todo files contain long markdown with many headings, the missing TOC makes them harder to navigate. **Fix:** Either add TOC injection to `todos.ts` and `archive.ts` (matching the `knowledge.ts` pattern), or explicitly document the decision to omit TOC from those pages. --- ### Issue 6 — MEDIUM: `config.test.ts` has no test for `report_base_url` field **File:** `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` The `Config` interface in `src/heartbeat/config.ts` was extended with `report_base_url: string` (line 52), a default constant `DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''` (line 27), and parsing logic (lines 251–254). However, `config.test.ts` has comprehensive test sections for `generate_report`/`ai_summary_model` (lines 274–325) and for `locale`/`gh_pages_branch`/`cname`/`webhook_url` (lines 331–418), but **no corresponding test section for `report_base_url`**. The `saveConfig` roundtrip test (line 388–409) does not include `report_base_url` in the `original` object literal, meaning it also doesn't verify that value round-trips through serialization. **Fix:** Add a test section for `report_base_url` that covers: default value (`''`), parsing from TOML, graceful handling of a non-string value, and the round-trip via `saveConfig`/`loadConfig`. --- ### Issue 7 — MEDIUM: `dashboard.ts` has a dead private function `transcriptHref` **File:** `src/report/render/dashboard.ts` lines 147–149 ```ts function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string { return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m) } ``` This private function wraps `transcriptUrlFromRoot` but is never called. All dashboard rendering uses `transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)` directly (line 109). The function was likely a leftover from refactoring that moved the URL helper to `layout.ts`. **Fix:** Remove the dead `transcriptHref` function from `dashboard.ts`. --- ### Issue 8 — MEDIUM: `search.ts` — `buildSearchIndex` double-serializes `entry` to compute byte count **File:** `src/report/render/search.ts` lines 36–44 ```ts const entryBytes = JSON.stringify(entry).length if (totalBytes + entryBytes > MAX_INDEX_BYTES) { const remaining = MAX_INDEX_BYTES - totalBytes - (entryBytes - snippet.length) - 10 if (remaining < 20) break snippet = snippet.slice(0, remaining) entry.snippet = snippet // ← mutates entry (violates immutability rule) } totalBytes += JSON.stringify(entry).length // ← second serialization of same entry entries.push(entry) ``` Two problems: (1) The entry is serialized twice — `JSON.stringify(entry)` is called once to measure `entryBytes`, then again at line 44 after `snippet` may have been truncated. This is a small performance overhead but indicative of unclear logic. (2) `entry.snippet = snippet` mutates the `entry` object after construction, violating the immutability principle established in project coding guidelines. **Fix:** Compute the byte count once after any truncation, and construct the entry with the final snippet rather than mutating it post-hoc. --- ### Issue 9 — MEDIUM: `layout.ts` — `htmlShell` renders reader-mode button outside content area unconditionally when `readerMode` is not explicitly `false` **File:** `src/report/render/layout.ts` lines 303–331 ```ts const readerBtn = opts.readerMode !== false ? `

'Dashboard', content, nav, '', lang) 26→} 27→ 28→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 29→// Internal renderers 30→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 31→ 32→function renderPageHeader(stats: ReportStats, t?: Translations): string { 33→ const from = stats.dateRange.from.slice(0, 10) || '—' 34→ const to = stats.dateRange.to.slice(0, 10) || '—' 35→ const title = t?.dashboard.title ?? 'Memory Dashboard' 36→ const subtitle = (t?.dashboard.subtitle ?? 'Activity from {from} to {to}') 37→ .replace('{from}', from) 38→ .replace('{to}', to) 39→ return `

'Tool Events' }, 50→ { value: fmtNum(stats.activeDays), label: t?.dashboard.activeDays ?? 'Active Days' }, 51→ ] 52→ 53→ const html = cards 54→ .map( 55→ c => `
56→
${escHtml(c.label)}
57→
${c.value}
58→
`, 59→ ) 60→ .join('') 61→ 62→ return `
${html}
` 63→} 64→ 65→function renderCharts(stats: ReportStats): string { 66→ const heatmap = renderHeatmap(stats.dayBuckets) 67→ const doughnut = renderClientDoughnut(stats.clientCounts) 68→ const line = renderWeeklyLine(stats.weekBuckets) 69→ const bar = renderToolBar(stats.toolCounts) 70→ 71→ return `
72→
73→
Activity (last 365 days)
74→ ${heatmap} 75→
76→
77→
Client Distribution
78→ ${doughnut} 79→
80→
81→

84→

88→

'Recent Sessions' 97→ const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client' 98→ const titleLabel = 'Title' 99→ const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date' 100→ const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs' 101→ const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools' 102→ 103→ if (recent.length === 0) { 104→ return `

${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}

` 105→ } 106→ 107→ const rows = recent 108→ .map(m => { 109→ const url = transcriptUrlFromRoot(m) 110→ const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10) 111→ const badge = clientBadge(m.client) 112→ const msgs = m.message_count 113→ const tools = m.tool_event_count 114→ const summary = '' 115→ const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${msgs} msgs` 116→ return ` 117→ ${badge} 118→ ${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)} 119→ ${escHtml(date)} 120→ ${msgs} 121→ ${tools} 122→` 123→ }) 124→ .join('') 125→ 126→ return `

${escHtml(heading)}

127→
128→ 129→ 130→ 131→ 132→ 133→ 134→${rows} 135→
${escHtml(clientLabel)}Title${escHtml(dateLabel)}${escHtml(msgsLabel)}${escHtml(toolsLabel)}
136→
` 137→} 138→ 139→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 140→// Helpers 141→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 142→ 143→function fmtNum(n: number): string { 144→ return n.toLocaleString('en-US') 145→} 146→ 147→function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string { 148→ return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m) 149→} 150→ 151→function detectLang(t: Translations): string { 152→ // Heuristic: check if nav.dashboard is Chinese 153→ return /[\u4e00-\u9fff]/.test(t.nav.dashboard) ? 1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import {
     3→  mkdtempSync,
     4→  mkdirSync,
     5→  writeFileSync,
     6→  readFileSync,
     7→  rmSync,
     8→  existsSync,
     9→  readdirSync,
    10→} from 'node:fs'
    11→import { join } from 'node:path'
    12→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
    13→import { buildReport, ensureGitignore, parseMessagesFromMarkdown } from '../../src/report/build.js'
    14→
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→// Setup
    17→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→
    19→let tmpDir: string
    20→
    21→beforeEach(() => {
    22→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'build-test-'))
    23→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    24→})
    25→
    26→afterEach(() => {
    27→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    28→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    29→})
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// ensureGitignore
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→describe('ensureGitignore', () => {
    36→  it('creates .gitignore with entry if missing', () => {
    37→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    38→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    39→    expect(existsSync(path)).toBe(true)
    40→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    41→    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
    42→  })
    43→
    44→  it('appends to existing .gitignore', () => {
    45→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    46→    writeFileSync(path, 'node_modules/\ndist/\n', 'utf-8')
    47→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    48→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    49→    expect(content).toContain('node_modules/')
    50→    expect(content).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
    51→  })
    52→
    53→  it('does not add duplicate entry', () => {
    54→    const path = join(tmpDir, '.gitignore')
    55→    writeFileSync(path, 'Memory/07_reports/\n', 'utf-8')
    56→    ensureGitignore(tmpDir, 'Memory/07_reports/')
    57→    const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    58→    const count = (content.match(/Memory\/07_reports\//g) ?? []).length
    59→    expect(count).toBe(1)
    60→  })
    61→})
    62→
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→// parseMessagesFromMarkdown
    65→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→
    67→describe('parseMessagesFromMarkdown', () => {
    68→  it('parses messages from clean markdown format', () => {
    69→    const md = `---
    70→client: codex
    71→---
    72→
    73→# Test Session
    74→
    75→## Messages
    76→
    77→### 1. user
    78→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`
    79→
    80→Hello, how are you?
    81→
    82→### 2. assistant
    83→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\`
    84→
    85→I'm doing well, thank you!
    86→`
    87→    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)
    88→    expect(messages).toHaveLength(2)
    89→    expect(messages[0]!.role).toBe('user')
    90→    expect(messages[0]!.text).toContain('Hello')
    91→    expect(messages[1]!.role).toBe('assistant')
    92→    expect(messages[1]!.text).toContain("I'm doing well")
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  it('returns empty array when no ## Messages section', () => {
    96→    const md = '# Title\n\nSome content.'
    97→    expect(parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)).toEqual([])
    98→  })
    99→
   100→  it('parses timestamps', () => {
   101→    const md = `\n## Messages\n\n### 1. user\n- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`\n\nHello`
   102→    const messages = parseMessagesFromMarkdown(md)
   103→    expect(messages[0]!.timestamp).toBe('2026-03-10T10:00:00Z')
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→
   107→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→// buildReport integration test
   109→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→describe('buildReport', () => {
   112→  function scaffoldMemory(root: string): void {
   113→    // Create directory structure
   114→    const manifestsDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests', 'codex', '2026', '03')
   115→    const cleanDir = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean', 'codex', '2026', '03')
   116→    mkdirSync(manifestsDir, { recursive: true })
   117→    mkdirSync(cleanDir, { recursive: true })
   118→
   119→    const stem = 'test-session__deadbeef'
   120→    const manifest = {
   121→      client: 'codex',
   122→      project: 'test',
   123→      session_id: 'test-session-001',
   124→      raw_sha256: 'deadbeef' + '0'.repeat(56),
   125→      title: 'Integration Test Session',
   126→      started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
   127→      imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
   128→      cwd: '/home/user/project',
   129→      branch: 'main',
   130→      raw_source_path: '/src/session.jsonl',
   131→      raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
   132→      global_raw_path: '',
   133→      global_clean_path: '',
   134→      global_manifest_path: '',
   135→      repo_raw_path: '',
   136→      repo_clean_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`,
   137→      repo_manifest_path: `Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2026/03/${stem}.json`,
   138→      message_count: 2,
   139→      tool_event_count: 0,
   140→      cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
   141→      repo_mirror_enabled: true,
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    writeFileSync(
   145→      join(manifestsDir, `${stem}.json`),
   146→      JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2),
   147→      'utf-8',
   148→    )
   149→
   150→    const cleanMd = `---
   151→client: codex
   152→---
   153→
   154→# Integration Test Session
   155→
   156→## Messages
   157→
   158→### 1. user
   159→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:00Z\`
   160→
   161→Build the report system.
   162→
   163→### 2. assistant
   164→- Timestamp: \`2026-03-10T10:00:05Z\`
   165→
   166→I'll help with that!
   167→`
   168→    writeFileSync(join(cleanDir, `${stem}.md`), cleanMd, 'utf-8')
   169→  }
   170→
   171→  it('creates all expected HTML files', async () => {
   172→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   173→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   174→
   175→    await buildReport({
   176→      root: tmpDir,
   177→      output,
   178→      noAi: true,
   179→      pdf: false,
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   183→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'transcripts', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   184→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'goals', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   185→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'knowledge', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   186→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'search.html'))).toBe(true)
   187→  })
   188→
   189→  it('generates individual transcript HTML with SVG charts in dashboard', async () => {
   190→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   191→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   192→
   193→    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })
   194→
   195→    // Dashboard should contain SVG
   196→    const dashHtml = readFileSync(join(output, 'index.html'), 'utf-8')
   197→    expect(dashHtml).toContain('<svg')
   198→
   199→    // Individual transcript should exist
   200→    const transcriptDir = join(output, 'transcripts', 'codex')
   201→    expect(existsSync(transcriptDir)).toBe(true)
   202→    const files = readdirSync(transcriptDir)
   203→    expect(files.some(f => f.endsWith('.html'))).toBe(true)
   204→  })
   205→
   206→  it('creates .gitignore entry for Memory/07_reports/', async () => {
   207→    scaffoldMemory(tmpDir)
   208→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   209→
   210→    await buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false })
   211→
   212→    const gitignore = readFileSync(join(tmpDir, '.gitignore'), 'utf-8')
   213→    expect(gitignore).toContain('Memory/07_reports/')
   214→  })
   215→
   216→  it('handles empty Memory directory gracefully', async () => {
   217→    // Create root but no transcripts
   218→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory'), { recursive: true })
   219→    const output = join(tmpDir, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   220→
   221→    await expect(
   222→      buildReport({ root: tmpDir, output, noAi: true, pdf: false }),
   223→    ).resolves.toBeUndefined()
   224→
   225→    expect(existsSync(join(output, 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
   226→  })
   227→})
   228→

input.old_string:
function slugifyHeading(text: string): string {
  return text
    .toLowerCase()
    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, '')   // remove non-word chars except hyphen
    .trim()
    .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-')   // spaces/underscores → hyphen
    .replace(/-+/g, '-')       // collapse multiple hyphens
}
input.new_string:
function slugifyHeading(text: string): string {
  return text
    .toLowerCase()
    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, '')   // remove non-word chars except hyphen
    .trim()
    .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-')   // spaces/underscores → hyphen
    .replace(/-+/g, '-')       // collapse multiple hyphens
}

/**
 * Strip common markdown inline markers to get plain text.
 * Used to match TOC entries against rendered HTML heading content.
 */
function stripInlineMarkdown(text: string): string {
  return text
    .replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1')          // **bold**
    .replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, '$1')               // *italic*
    .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1')                 // `code`
    .replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)/g, '$1')    // [link](url) → text
    .trim()
}

/**

 * Used to match TOC entries against rendered HTML heading content.
 */
function stripInlineMarkdown(text: string): string {
  return text
    .replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1')          // **bold**
    .replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, '$1')               // *italic*
    .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1')                 // `code`

}

cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(report): Report v2 Phase 2 — TOC, Breadcrumb, Callouts, RSS, Tags, Search filters, OG Meta, Mermaid

New files:
- src/report/render/toc.ts: h2/h3 TOC extraction, collapsible nav, heading ID injection
- src/report/render/breadcrumb.ts: accessible breadcrumb nav
- src/report/render/rss.ts: RSS 2.0 feed with RFC 2822 dates, XML escaping, 100-item cap
- src/report/tags.ts: AI tag extraction with SHA256 cache, renderTagBadges()

Feature additions:
- Callout syntax (> [!note/tip/warning/...]) with per-type emoji and color
- Mermaid CDN conditional injection (only pages containing ```mermaid blocks)
- Search page: client + project <select> filter dropdowns, combined AND logic
- Tag badges on session list (AI-extracted, cached, noAi-safe)
- OG Meta: og:title/type/description/url in htmlShell, transcript pages pass reportBaseUrl
- TOC integrated into knowledge/goals/todos/archive pages (all 4 consistent)
- Reader Mode: hides sidebar/topbar/breadcrumb, localStorage-persisted
- View Transitions API: cross-page fade animation, progressive enhancement
- Sidebar: emoji icon mode (768-1024px), collapse toggle with localStorage
- CSS: tag pills, search filter selects, callout variants, TOC, breadcrumb, reader mode, view transitions

Config / CLI:
- report_base_url config field (RSS <link> + OG og:url)
- --locale and --report-base-url CLI flags on `memorytree report build`

Bug fixes (review):
- rss.ts: toRfc2822 checks isNaN(d.getTime()) — Invalid Date no longer silent
- toc.ts: injectHeadingIds regex [\s\S]*? handles headings with inline HTML
- search.ts: remove entry mutation, single JSON.stringify
- breadcrumb.ts: remove trailing space in urlless non-last items
- dashboard.ts: remove dead transcriptHref function
- build.ts: remove unused renderTagBadges import
- build.test.ts: remove non-existent pdf field from BuildReportOptions calls
- tags.ts: use escHtml from layout.ts, add t.length <= 50 guard

Tests: 611 passing (was 551), +60 new tests across toc, breadcrumb, rss, tags, config
EOF
)"

input.command:
cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && gh pr create --base fix/codex-parser-duplicates-and-injection --title "feat(report): Report v2 — Quartz 对标全功能升级" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

两阶段完整实现 MemoryTree Report v2，功能对标 Quartz，支持 GitHub Pages 部署。

### Phase 1（commit 97ae58d）
- **侧边栏导航**：240px fixed sidebar，emoji 图标，主题切换（dark/light），localStorage 持久化
- **i18n**：`loadLocale(locale)` 构建时单语言，内置 `en` / `zh-CN`
- **新页面**：Graph（force-directed 知识图谱）、Projects、Todos、Archive
- **部署**：`gh-pages` 分支推送、Webhook 通知（飞书/Telegram/Discord/Slack）
- **Popover 预览**：session 链接 hover 显示摘要

### Phase 2（commit c753bbe）
- **TOC**：h2/h3 可折叠目录，锚点平滑滚动，支持内联 HTML 标题（`**bold**`）
- **Breadcrumb**：`renderBreadcrumb()` accessible `<nav aria-label="breadcrumb">`
- **Callouts**：`> [!note/tip/warning/...]` 语法，颜色左边框 + emoji 图标
- **Mermaid**：条件注入 CDN script，仅含 ` ```mermaid ` 的页面注入，其余页面零开销
- **RSS 2.0**：`feed.xml`，RFC 2822 日期，XML 转义，100 条上限
- **OG Meta**：`og:title/type/description/url`，`reportBaseUrl` 为空时优雅跳过
- **Search 过滤**：client + project 双 `<select>` 下拉，AND 逻辑
- **Tag 系统**：AI 提取 3-5 关键词，SHA256 缓存，noAi 安全，pill badges
- **Reader Mode**：隐藏 sidebar/topbar/meta，localStorage 持久化
- **View Transitions API**：跨页淡入淡出，渐进增强，~5 行 CSS + JS
- **侧边栏折叠**：`«` 按钮 + localStorage，768-1024px 图标模式
- **CLI flags**：`--locale`、`--report-base-url`

### Review 修复
两轮 code review 发现的所有 CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW 问题均已修复（共 13 项）。

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run build` — ⚡️ Build success
- [x] `npm test` — **611 tests passing**, 0 failures（较 Phase 1 +60 新测试）
- [x] 新增测试文件：`toc.test.ts`、`breadcrumb.test.ts`、`rss.test.ts`、`tags.test.ts`
- [x] `config.test.ts` 补充 `report_base_url` 5 条测试
- [ ] 手动验证：`memorytree report build --root <repo> --no-ai --locale zh-CN`
- [ ] 手动验证：`memorytree report serve` 预览所有页面

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

确认了：**`markdownToHtml` 完全没有实现 `![alt](url)` 图片语法**。

当前 `applyInline` 只处理：bold / italic / inline code / `[text](url)` 链接。`![alt](url)` 会被当成普通链接处理（`[alt](url)` 部分），前面的 `!` 原样输出，图片完全不渲染。

---

所以你提到的"知识图片"可以有两种理解：

**A. Markdown 图片 `![alt](url)`** — 用户在 `Memory/04_knowledge/` 的 md 文件里引用了本地或远程图片，但 HTML 报告里看不到，只看到 `![alt]` 文字

**B. Graph 页面的知识图谱** — `graph.html` 的 force-directed 可视化，这个已经实现了

你说的是 **A（markdown 图片渲染）** 还是 **B（知识图谱）**？还是另有所指？
